text.title: GitLab Boards — Agentic Workflows and Gotchas
text.summary: Recommended subagents, prompt patterns, and failure modes for agent-driven GitLab board operations.

list.item: board-author (sonnet) — synthesize board lists and scoped labels from a process YAML description.
triage-agent (sonnet) — classify and route incoming issues by type, priority, and component; never auto-close.
mr-issue-linker (haiku) — enforce "Closes #N" discipline and matching scoped labels before merge.
cycle-time-watcher (haiku, scheduled) — pull lead/cycle time from issue events, alert on drift.
webhook-router (haiku) — receive GitLab events and route to the appropriate downstream agent.

example.code: You are board-author. Inputs: process.yaml (stages with WIP limits, filters, label scheme). Emit (a) scoped labels to create with "::" syntax, (b) an array of board lists with order + WIP limit, (c) a smoke-test plan that creates a test issue and walks it through every transition. Return STRICT JSON. Refuse if any stage lacks an exit criterion.

You are triage-agent. Given an incoming issue (title, body, project context, existing labels), output: { "type": "bug|feature|tech-debt|docs|question", "priority": "critical|high|medium|low", "component": "<from project list>", "workflow": "ready|backlog", "reasons": [...] } Cite spans from the body that support each tag. Do not invent components not in the project's list.

antipattern.description: Using Personal Access Tokens with wide scopes for bot operations.
antipattern.reason: Prefer Group or Project Access Tokens scoped to "api" + "write_repository" only. Wide-scope PATs create blast radius on compromise.

antipattern.description: Bot-created issues notifying all project subscribers.
antipattern.reason: Bot users create issues as bot-owned; downstream notifications fan out to thousands. Use a dedicated bot user with notifications muted.

antipattern.description: Assuming free-tier feature parity for iteration cadences.
antipattern.reason: Iteration cadence creation requires Premium. Agents written against Premium features break on free-tier customer projects.

rule.statement: Apply human-in-the-loop checkpoints for: board structure changes, label-scheme changes, auto-close rules, and group-level moves.
rule.rationale: These operations affect all team members simultaneously and are difficult to reverse cleanly.

text.reference: Seed standard scoped labels on a project via REST API
Bug report issue template with quick-action footer
Feature request issue template with AC checklist
